# Pylon vs Plain (2026): B2B Slack Support Tools Compared

## Summary

Both Plain and Pylon are purpose-built for B2B companies managing customer support through Slack Connect channels. They represent opposite philosophies: Plain is API-first and developer-oriented; Pylon is workflow-builder and operations-oriented. The comparison site supp.support provides a detailed 2026 pricing table and feature matrix based on publicly available data and user reports.

**Pricing (as of 2026):**

| Plan | Pylon | Plain |
|---|---|---|
| Entry | $59/seat/mo (Starter, 3-seat min) | $35/seat/mo (Foundation, 1-seat min) |
| Mid | $89/seat/mo (Professional) | $269/mo base + $89/seat (Horizon) |
| Enterprise | $139/seat/mo (7-seat min) | Custom (Frontier) |
| AI cost | $50/seat/mo add-on | Included in all plans (Ari) |
| Free trial | None | 14 days, no card required |

A 3-person team entry cost: Pylon $177/mo vs. Plain $105/mo. Plain's entry price is 40% lower and includes AI on every plan. Pylon's entry includes a built-in knowledge base and help center, which Plain gates behind the $269/mo Horizon plan.

**Feature comparison highlights:**

- Slack integration: Pylon is deeper (bulk messaging, in-Slack CSAT, multi-workspace); Plain is bidirectional sync with message grouping
- API: Pylon is REST; Plain is GraphQL with full internal parity and no rate limits
- AI agent: Pylon sells AI ($50/seat/mo add-on); Plain includes Ari on every plan
- Custom AI agent (BYOA): Plain only (Frontier tier)
- SLAs: Available on Pylon all plans; Plain gates SLAs behind Horizon ($269/mo)
- CSAT in Slack: Pylon only
- Bulk messaging: Pylon only
- Account intelligence: Pylon only ($10/account add-on)
- Discord: Plain only (Horizon+)
- UI speed: Plain is keyboard-driven and noticeably faster (instant vs. 2-5s load times in Pylon)

**Customer rosters (2026):**

- Pylon: Deel, Hightouch, Merge, Temporal, AssemblyAI, Honeycomb, Lumos, Vellum. 750+ customers, $51M raised (a16z, Bain). No free trial.
- Plain: Sanity, Laravel, Stytch, Raycast, Clerk, Mintlify, Railway, Resend, n8n, CodeSandbox. $21M raised (Battery Ventures, Index). Developer-tools companies.

## Key takeaways

- Plain wins on price, API depth, trial availability, and AI inclusion; Pylon wins on Slack-native features (bulk messaging, CSAT), knowledge base out-of-the-box, and account intelligence
- Pylon is better for ops-led support teams; Plain is better for engineering-led support teams
- If the team has <5 people and needs to try before buying, Plain is clearly the lower-risk entry
- SLAs are not available on Plain until the $269/mo Horizon plan — a critical gating factor for startups that need SLA enforcement from day one
- Both tools require a Slack-centric customer base to justify the cost; neither is suitable as a primary support tool for non-Slack customers
